rationale_for_baseline_tier: Small-volume bright-red-blood-and-mucus proctitis bleeding with stable posture (no syncope), afebrile, is a proctitis flare that can be managed by restarting topical mesalazine with prompt GP/gastroenterology follow-up — a T2 baseline. The mandatory escalations are for a major bleed (large-volume/postural syncope) or sepsis/surgical abdomen.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Large, cup-sized volumes of blood or clots, or feeling faint/fainting on standing
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates a haemodynamically significant lower-GI bleed.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you pass large, cup-sized volumes of blood or clots, or feel faint or actually faint when you stand up, go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: High fever with shaking chills, or severe constant generalised abdominal pain/rigidity
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a high fever with shakes, or severe constant tummy pain with a hard belly, go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Restart maintenance therapy; ensure gastroenterology linkage.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a flare of your ulcerative proctitis — the inflamed rectum bleeds easily and gives you that constant urge, but small amounts of blood and mucus look much worse than the amount you're losing, and you're stable. Restart your mesalazine suppository tonight (lie on your left side), use a heat pack for the cramps, and book GP/gastroenterology review this week. Go to ED if you pass large cups of blood or clots, feel faint on standing, or get a high fever or severe constant tummy pain.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP/gastroenterology within 7 days; ED for major-bleed/sepsis features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend ED overnight if a major bleed or sepsis feature develops.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of an ulcerative proctitis flare (lapsed mesalazine); major LGIB and sepsis/surgical abdomen screened negative (small-volume bleeding, stable posture, afebrile); topical mesalazine restarted; major-bleed/sepsis safety-net given; maintenance review recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring rectal bleeding without assessing volume/haemodynamics or giving a major-bleed safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A significant lower-GI bleed or toxic colitis is missed and the patient deteriorates.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine review is acceptable only with a major-bleed/sepsis safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for small-volume proctitis bleeding with stable posture is over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot measure postural BP/haemoglobin or examine the rectum remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Use bleeding volume and postural symptoms to gauge significance, restart topical mesalazine, and give a major-bleed/sepsis safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either over-triage small-volume bleeding to ED or reassure without any bleeding/haemodynamic assessment.
